text: Azabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes (aza-BCHs) and bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes (BCPs) have recently emerged as $sp^3$-rich core motifs in medicinal chemistry. Bioisosteric replacement of the traditional flat aromatic rings with these novel three-dimensional structures enables the creation of metabolically stable drugs and leads that preclude potential flatland degradation. Therefore, direct methods for single-atom scaffold hop from aza-BCHs to BCPs would enable precise access to these valuable $sp^3$-based structural cores. Here, we present a formal nitrogen-atom deletion transformation from aza-BCH to BCP cores by harnessing photochemical [2+2] cycloadditions for the convenient preparation of multifunctionalized aza-BCHs followed by catalyst-free and mild deamination. This resultant strategy provides efficient access to a wide range of substituted BCPs, a structural class of core motif that is particularly challenging to synthesize directly. Most importantly, this transformation serves as a universal platform that is applicable for the synthesis of various bioactive and pharmaceutical-inspired bridge functionalized bicyclo-pentane cores.
